q: Are these books good for neurodivergent or autistic kids?
a: Yes. Our Just the Way You Are series was created specifically to celebrate neurodivergent kids — it uses identity-first, deficit-free language and was inspired by a real boy named Clayton. Many families read them with autistic, ADHD, and sensory-sensitive children.

q: What age are these picture books for?
a: They're written for roughly ages 2–7 — toddlers, preschoolers, and early elementary readers — as read-alouds and early independent reads. The gentle ones (like Good Night Dinosaur) work from the youngest ages; the adventure titles suit slightly older listeners.
Every title is available on Amazon. Just tap “Buy on Amazon” on any book and you'll go straight to its product page. All books are premium full color, 8.5×8.5, and $13.99.

q: Do you offer bulk, library, or classroom orders?
a: Yes — we love getting these books into classrooms and libraries. Use the contact page to ask about wholesale, library, or classroom pricing and we'll get right back to you.
These are picture books meant to be read aloud, with simple, rhythmic text. Early readers can begin reading them independently around kindergarten and first grade.
Isaiah Hartwell is the pen name behind Made Out of Clay Productions, an independent imprint creating warm, inclusive picture books across three series.

q: Are the books part of a series?
a: Yes — there are three: At Sunny's Table (a gentle sloth family), Just the Way You Are (neurodiversity and inclusion), and The Kingdom of Judea (a lion-kingdom adventure). You can read any book on its own.
